watchlist.intro: Each watchlist has its own schedule — fixed interval, daily or weekly — and is checked right away when created. New results above the seeders minimum are pushed to the configured ntfy.sh topic. To receive on your phone: install ntfy.sh and subscribe to the topic.
watchlist.hits_empty: No detections yet. The first check runs right after creating; then it follows the schedule.
watchlist.ai_placeholder: Describe it freely (e.g. every Monday at 9am)
watchlist.ai_unclear: Couldn't read that as a schedule — try rephrasing (e.g. "every 3 hours")
watchlist.auto_download_help: New torrents that pass the filters are queued for download automatically (max. 3 per check). The first check only records the current results — nothing is downloaded retroactively.
watchlist.auto_downloaded_hit: Automatically added to the download queue
watchlist.delete_message: Delete this watchlist? All "already seen" records will be lost.
watchlist.title: Watchlists
watchlist.empty_hint: Create one to get a push when new torrents appear.

page.removeDownloadMessage: Stop and remove this download from the queue? The streaming/transcode session opened by Play is also ended.

page.viewFilesTitle: Opens the player: plays the main file and lists the rest of the torrent

page.stopStreamingTitle: Ends and removes the torrent from streaming (different from Pause; the downloaded cache is not deleted)

queueCard.title: Downloads Queue
queueCard.intro: How many downloads run at the same time and how the queue behaves. Streaming (play now) doesn't count toward the limit.
queueCard.maxActiveHint: Server cap: maximum downloading at the same time across ALL users.
queueCard.perUserHint: Maximum EACH user downloads at the same time. 0 = no limit (only the global cap applies).
queueCard.stallHint: Time with no progress AND no seeds before moving to the end of the queue.
queueCard.maxStallsHint: After N rounds without downloading, pauses the download.
queueCard.agingHint: Every X min in the queue a low-priority item moves up (anti-starvation). 0 disables it.
queueCard.rotationHint: When a download runs out of seeds, it looks up other sources for the same content on Jackett and alternates between them (round-robin).
queueCard.arrHint: Downloads coming from Sonarr/Radarr (via Transmission RPC) are written straight to {{path}} — the same tree Transmission uses — so the *arr apps import them as expected. Requires the shared directory (JACKUI_SHARED_DIR) configured.
queueCard.copyModeAuto: Automatic (recommended) — serializes on HDD, parallelizes on SSD
queueCard.copyModeHint: On a mechanical HDD, parallel copies compete for the disk head and get slow (seek thrashing). Automatic detects the destination disk and decides on its own.

inspect.sourcesEmpty: No alternative sources yet. When automatic rotation is on and the download runs out of seeds, other sources for the same content will appear here.
inspect.filesInactive: Torrent isn't active right now — file list unavailable. Try a recheck to re-attach.
inspect.downloadMissingTitle: Downloads (and moves to downloads on completion) all files still only streaming
inspect.adoptFileTitle: Download this file (moves to downloads on completion; reuses what's already cached)
inspect.deleteMessage: Delete download "{{name}}"? The file on disk is NOT removed.
inspect.sparseTitle: Sparse file — allocated bytes < declared size
inspect.torrentInactive: Torrent isn't active in the streamer right now (dropped after completion or not yet resolved). Swarm/speed data unavailable.
inspect.noTrackers: No tracker found. The torrent may have been added via .torrent without &tr= in the magnet.
inspect.trackersConfigured: Tracker servers configured for this torrent:
inspect.actionPlayDesc: Watch this complete file locally in the built-in player.
inspect.actionRecheckDesc: Re-hashes all pieces and resets progress so the worker reconciles with disk. Useful when you suspect corruption.
inspect.actionPromoteDesc: Moves the file to the shared library (JACKUI_SHARED_DIR). Optionally keep seeding.
inspect.actionStopSeedDesc: Drops the torrent from the streamer. The file on disk stays. The download row stays in the DB.
inspect.actionDeleteDesc: Removes the row from the DB. The file on disk is NOT deleted (use promote or delete it manually). The torrent keeps seeding if still active.

peers.inactive: Torrent inactive — it's not in the swarm right now, so there's no upload or peers. Play or download to reactivate (or enable the tracker in Settings → continuous seeding).
peers.footerNote: "Sending"/"receiving" are inferred from current rates — the lib doesn't expose choke/interest state.

ai.title: AI Identification
ai.disabled: Disabled — set <c>GROQ_API_KEY</c>, <c>OPENROUTER_API_KEY</c> or <c>OLLAMA_BASE_URL</c> to enable AI title cleanup.
ai.confirm_run_message: Running the benchmark{{provider}} consumes model quota (it tests each model several times). Continue?
ai.run_done: Benchmark{{provider}} finished — chain adopted by best score.
ai.run_done_background: Benchmark finished in the background — results updated.
ai.run_timeout_still_running: The request timed out, but the benchmark keeps running on the server — following the progress…
ai.run_failed_timeout: Failed (it may have timed out; reload to see the saved result).
ai.cancel_title: Stops the running benchmark — whatever was already measured stays saved.
ai.cancel_done: Benchmark cancelled — whatever had already been measured was kept.
ai.cancel_nothing: Nothing to cancel (the benchmark had already finished).
ai.rerun_incomplete_title: Re-runs only the models cut by rate limits. Run it later (even the next day) to get outside the limit window.
ai.rerun_incomplete_left: Missing ones re-run — {{count}} still incomplete (try outside the rate-limit window).
ai.rerun_incomplete_all: Missing ones re-run — all complete now.
ai.cost_saved: Costs saved — run the benchmark to apply them to the ranking.
ai.explain: Current chain: {{chain}}. The benchmark measures the full extraction (title + year for movies, series + season/episode for TV) and the latency per model, computes the composite score (accuracy ÷ √latency ÷ (1 + cost/1M)) and reorders the chain. Latency is the <s>median</s> of the calls (discounting model load time); <s>cost</s> ($/1M tokens, in USD) penalizes expensive models, so free/cheap rises. Adjust the cost cap, energy price and GPU power below — local models aren't free (they spend energy: latency × tokens × power × price).
ai.cases_help: Test cases (one per line: <c>file.name => Expected Label</c>). The label splits the structure: <c>Movie - Year</c>, <c>Series - S03E07</c>, <c>Series - E01</c>, or just the title. Prefix the task with <c>[schedule]</c> or <c>[identify]</c> (no prefix = rename); schedule uses <c>weekly:1:7:0</c> / <c>daily:21:30</c> / <c>interval:180</c>.

stream.saved_restart: Saved. Some changes (storage/connections/cache) only apply after restarting the container.
stream.title: Streamer & Performance
stream.seed_trackers_label: Trackers to keep seeding (one per line)
stream.seed_trackers_help: Torrents whose announce matches any line (substring, case-insensitive) are NOT dropped after use — they stay in the swarm uploading, and resume seeding on their own after a restart. Ideal for private trackers where ratio matters. Empty = no one is kept (default behavior). Public ones keep being dropped normally.
stream.readahead_hint: Buffer read ahead per session. Larger = smoother playback on slow network/disk, but more RAM per stream. Applies on the next play.
stream.cache_limit_hint: When exceeded, the oldest inactive entries are removed (favorites protected).
stream.storage_mmap: mmap — maps into memory (page cache; faster seeks)
stream.field_zero_hint: Field at 0 = use the library default (shown in the placeholder).

cache.clear_all_message: Delete ALL streaming cache files? This interrupts any active torrent.

cache.limit_note: — when the limit is exceeded, the oldest inactive entries are removed automatically.
cache.play_title: Play — reactivates the torrent if needed
cache.favorite_no_remove: Favorites can't be removed — unfavorite first in the player
